skill.name: voc-research
skill.description: Extract exact Voice of Customer quotes from forums, reviews, social media, and support sources for copywriting and messaging research.
skill.summary: Find, categorize, and preserve real customer language for conversion copywriting research.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick and weak cryptography findings are false positives in markdown instructions, not executable code. The skill legitimately uses web research and write-capable tools, so publication is acceptable with low-risk disclosure.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged inline and fenced markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution. These lines document storage names, integration paths, and an output example; they are not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are markdown formatting and an example block. No shell invocation, interpolation, or runtime execution path appears in those lines.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on text that describes the skill and quote categories. No hash function, cipher, crypto library, or credential handling appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural-language research instructions only. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: Broad Research Tool Permissions
low_findings.description: The skill declares web, write, and Bash-capable tools for research workflows. This is not malicious, but users should review collected quotes before storing or sharing customer data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The declared tools and data sources clearly enable network research and storage. The context is legitimate VOC research, with no evidence of exfiltration or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams need real customer language before writing copy. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code collect exact quotes and organize them into messaging insights.
actual_capabilities: Search public forums, review sites, and social sources for customer comments.
Extract exact quotes without paraphrasing or rewriting customer language.
Categorize quotes into pain points, symptoms, dream outcomes, failed solutions, and buying decisions.
Identify repeated quote patterns and mark high-frequency themes as gold insights.
Preserve source context, dates, typos, and slang for research traceability.
limitations: It depends on accessible source material and cannot bypass private communities.
It does not verify whether every public comment reflects a real customer.
It may require human review before using quotes in published marketing.
It does not include a dedicated scraper or database implementation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Landing Page Copy
use_cases.description: Collect customer phrases before writing headlines, objections, and benefit statements.

use_cases.title: Map Product Pain Points
use_cases.description: Group real complaints and symptoms into themes for product positioning work.

use_cases.title: Build Client Messaging Research
use_cases.description: Create a structured VOC brief during onboarding or campaign planning.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Customer Quotes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner research for one product category.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use voc-research to find exact customer quotes about [product category]. Focus on pain points and include the source for each quote.

prompt_templates.title: Organize Quotes by Category
prompt_templates.scenario: Categorize existing quotes into the skill taxonomy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use voc-research to categorize these customer quotes into pain points, symptoms, dream outcomes, failed solutions, and buying decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Review Sources
prompt_templates.prompt: Use voc-research to compare customer language from Reddit, review sites, and forums for [audience]. Identify repeated themes across sources.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Messaging Insight Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use voc-research to build a VOC brief for [offer]. Include exact quotes, source context, recurring patterns, objections, and gold quote candidates.

output_examples.input: Research customer complaints about invoice software for freelancers.
output_examples.output: A categorized VOC table with exact quotes about late payments, follow-up fatigue, confusing reminders, and trust concerns.

output_examples.input: Find repeated language from users comparing two project management tools.
output_examples.output: A theme summary showing repeated phrases about setup friction, team adoption, notification overload, and switching triggers.

output_examples.input: Turn support ticket excerpts into messaging insights.
output_examples.output: A brief with verbatim support quotes grouped by pain point, desired outcome, failed workaround, and buying-decision language.

best_practices: Use public sources or approved internal data only.
Keep quotes verbatim and label every source clearly.
Review sensitive data before storing or publishing research.
anti_patterns: Do not invent customer quotes to fill gaps.
Do not remove typos or slang from quoted text.
Do not treat one source as proof of a market-wide pattern.

faq.question: What does this skill research?
faq.answer: It researches exact customer language from forums, reviews, social media, and approved support sources.

faq.question: Can it create customer quotes?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires real quotes and explicitly rejects AI-generated customer language.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it scrape private data?
faq.answer: No. It should use public sources or internal data that the user is authorized to provide.

faq.question: How are quotes organized?
faq.answer: Quotes are grouped into pain points, symptoms, dream outcomes, failed solutions, and buying decisions.

faq.question: Why are some quotes marked gold?
faq.answer: Gold quotes are phrases or themes that appear three or more times across sources.
